title: HTTP Caching, Freshness, Validators, Vary, and Invalidation · Automation and Data Client Systems · Code Recall
summary: A compliance analyst reconstructing a partial ingestion handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for http caching, freshness, validators, vary, and invalidation, producing spec-aware cache decision notebook. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers spec-aware cache decision notebook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction,…
objectives: Distinguish private, shared, gateway, browser, and application caches plus who may reuse stored responses. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Compute freshness from explicit directives, dates, age, and response context rather than wall-clock intuition. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Use ETag and Last-Modified validators with If-None-Match or If-Modified-Since and interpret 304 correctly. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.title: Python Private Shared Cache · changed case · Review Private Shared Cache
